text:
The future of artificial intelligence is a topic of much debate. Most current AI is narrow, designed for specific tasks, but many experts predict the arrival of artificial general intelligence, or AGI. AGI would be able to perform any intellectual task that a human can, marking a significant leap forward.

Potential benefits are enormous. AGI could solve complex problems, like curing diseases or managing climate change. However, there are also serious risks. A poorly designed AGI might act in ways we cannot predict or control. Timeline predictions vary wildly, from five to fifty years. While the exact date is unknown, it is clear that the development of AGI will be one of the most important events in human history.
